In America, obeying our laws is not optional—it’s a requirement for anyone seeking to stay here.

tweets.text:
A criminal alien in Sacramento, already ordered to leave the U.S. after racking up two DUIs, brazenly defied the law by re-entering the country under a completely different identity just months later! 

This blatant disregard for our nation's laws is unacceptable and will not be tolerated. @ICEgov picked him up at our office and will give him a safe and sober ride out of the country. Applying for a Green Card for an alien relative means all parties must be law abiding.

tweets.text:
R-1 religious workers no longer need to reside and be physically present outside the United States for one year at the end of their statutory five-year maximum admission period of stay to be eligible for readmission in R-1status.

This improvement will promote stability and minimize disruptions to the essential services provided by nonimmigrant religious workers in the United States.

Learn more: https://t.co/PNcEvu9LRM

tweets.text:
In 1996, an immigration judge denied a Chinese alien’s asylum claim and ordered him to leave the United States. 

He remained in our country illegally, but @ICEgov will finally get to deport him after being arrested at our West Palm Beach office. 

Applying for U.S. citizenship cannot have a murky path. When you have been lawfully denied multiple times, you can’t keep filing new applications.

tweets.text: We’ve been planning large-scale immigration fraud investigations for months. We looked at marriage and family-based immigration petitions, employment authorizations, and certain parole-related petitions. (3/6)

All USCIS fees must be paid via an ACH debit transaction from a U.S. bank using Form G-1650, or a credit/debit card using Form G-1450. Failure to use accepted payment methods will result in rejection.

tweets.text:
America needs vetted and legal farm workers to support our local agricultural businesses, and we’re working closely with @USDOL to modernize and streamline the H-2A process. 
 
Beginning Oct. 2, petitioners seeking unnamed beneficiaries will be able to electronically submit the newly developed Form I-129H2A after DOL issues a notice of acceptance on the H-2A temporary labor certification and before it is approved. USCIS does not approve any petitions until after DOL has approved the corresponding TLC. This change will allow USCIS to immediately begin the concurrent processing of electronically filed petitions with unnamed beneficiaries, allowing petitioners the flexibility to file a petition with USCIS sooner than previously permitted. 
 
When workers choose lawful pathways to working in the United States, it is beneficial for American businesses, the public, and alien workers. 
 
Learn more: https://t.co/x00OdmSSz7
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Applicants can now pay USCIS fees by ACH debit. Simply complete and sign our new Form G-1650, Authorization for ACH Transactions, and place the form on top of your application, petition, or request when you file.

We will continue to accept paper checks, money orders, and credit and debit transactions until Oct. 28, 2025. Starting Oct. 28, we will accept only ACH debit transactions using Form G-1650 or credit card payments using Form G-1450, Authorization for Credit Card Transactions.

To learn more, visit https://t.co/EkRIPuk1tW

tweets.text:
There's a reason we require biometrics for immigration applications: to verify identity. A woman from Mexico was ordered removed in 1998. 

27 years later, she applied for a Green Card under a new name. Our security checks uncovered the truth when her fingerprints and other identifying information matched. 

She was arrested by @ICEgov at our Fort Smith, Arkansas office. 

This is how we are disrupting fraud, restoring integrity in the immigration system, and making America safe again!

tweets.text: We are working with our state and local law enforcement partners to make America safe again. When an alien from Nigeria applied for a Green Card at our Orlando office, our robust screening showed that he had an active warrant for his arrest. He had failed to appear for his court date on other charges. We alerted @OrlandoPolice, and they arrested him. Being a guest in our country is a privilege that requires respecting and obeying our laws.

tweets.text:
We vet and screen all applications, including those of U.S. citizens when they petition for a family member. When this citizen petitioned for his alien wife, his background check came back with many red flags. It turns out he had DOZENS of active arrest warrants from four police departments in Ark.! The warrants were for:
▪️Two thefts
▪️Two DUIs
▪️Driving on a suspended license
▪️Child neglect
▪️25 charges of failure to appear
▪️16 criminal contempt of court charges
@FortSmithPD arrested him when he came with his spouse to our Fort Smith Ark. office for her Green Card interview. He will now face justice. Our duty to make America safe again goes beyond uncovering criminal aliens trying to access the immigration system.

tweets.text:
This Independence Day, we celebrate a safer America thanks to the Trump administration’s commitment to safeguard the American people and uphold our values. USCIS will commemorate the day with naturalization ceremonies across the country, welcoming aliens proving allegiance to the Constitution, laws, and founding principles of our country.

During this time, we must also recognize the Trump administration’s successful termination of the Biden-era CHNV and Temporary Protected Status programs—programs that poured in millions of unvetted illegal aliens into our country, made a mockery of our immigration system, and undermined the lawful immigration process. With this stark contrast, it’s clear we are delivering on the promise to restore integrity to our immigration system, make America safe again, and welcome those who respect and follow our laws.

Congratulations to all!

https://t.co/NVLve48Ydo

tweets.text:
An illegal alien from Haiti lied on his Green Card application. When USCIS discovered it, we put him in removal proceedings. An immigration judge revoked his Green Card and ordered him removed 7 months ago, but he didn't leave. 
Instead, this criminal applied for citizenship. When he showed up at our Jacksonville, Fla. office, @ICEgov showed up too, and he left in handcuffs.
You can't game the system. Under @POTUS and @Sec_Noem, our robust vetting works, and it's making America safe again!

media.alt_text:
Infographic. Text reads: Actions to Support Employment-Based Immigrants
Over the last three years, USCIS has:
Allocated each and every employment-based green card available under the law to an eligible applicant, so that no numbers were wasted.
Issued over 50% more green cards to workers and their families--that's well over 200,000 above the typical numbers.
Cut processing times by 40%, so that most eligible employment-based green card applicants are now waiting only about 6 months or less.

tweets.text_resolved:
#FilingTip: When filing Form I-140, be sure to provide correct fees and complete information.

Unless you qualify for a reduced fee, you must pay the new $600 Asylum Program Fee in addition to the $715 filing fee. learn more: https://www.uscis.gov/newsroom/alerts/guidance-on-paying-fees-and-completing-information-for-form-i-140-immigrant-petition-for-alien https://t.co/6zWTOmjNRf

media.alt_text:
When filing Form I-140, Immigrant Petition for Alien Workers, you must answer questions 5 and 6 in Part 1  

If you are a self-petitioner, answer “No” for question 5 and “Yes” for question 6.  

If you qualify as a tax-exempt nonprofit or government research organization and answer “Yes” for question 5, you do not pay any Asylum Program Fee. 

If you qualify as a small employer or self-petitioner with 25 or fewer employees (including zero employees) and answer “Yes” for question 6, you pay a reduced Asylum Program Fee of $300. 

If you answer “No” to both question 5 and 6, you must pay the full Asylum Program Fee of $600.

Scammers will:
-Try to confuse you by complicating the immigration process;
-Use social media, email, or phone calls to conduct their attacks; &
-Pose as government officials to manipulate the victim into giving out personal information.
Protect yourself: https://www.uscis.gov/avoid-scams

We will begin accepting requests from eligible individuals for this process on Aug. 19. More https://t.co/fJXb25hViI https://t.co/jJVIvYoWMk
tweets.text_resolved: Today @DHSgov announced a Federal Register notice to implement Keeping Families Together, a process for certain noncitizen spouses & stepchildren of U.S. citizens. We will begin accepting requests from eligible individuals for this process on Aug. 19. More https://www.uscis.gov/newsroom/news-releases/dhs-implements-keeping-families-together https://t.co/jJVIvYoWMk

media.alt_text: “Too often, noncitizen spouses of U.S. citizens – many of them mothers and fathers – live with uncertainty due to undue barriers in our immigration system. This process to keep U.S. families together will remove these undue barriers for those who would otherwise qualify to live and work lawfully in the United States, while also creating greater efficiencies in the immigration system, conducting effective screening and vetting, and focusing on noncitizens who contribute to and have longstanding connections within American communities across the country.” USCIS Director Ur M. Jaddou

media.alt_text: USCIS #IndependenceDay celebrations kicked into high gear onboard the “Big J” on July 4. We welcomed 42 #NewUSCitizens, including 8 Department of Defense #ServiceMembers from the U.S. Army, U.S. Army Reserve, NJ National Guard, U.S. Air Force and U.S. Coast Guard naturalized onboard Battleship New Jersey – the most-decorated battleship in U.S. Navy history. It was a fitting celebration of the anniversary of the Declaration of Independence, overlooking the skyline of Philadelphia – where the declaration was written 248 years ago.
